summary: RBAC Migration testing complete. All 184+ backend routes successfully migrated from legacy decorators to require_permission(Module, Action) system. Backend: 44/44 tests pass. Frontend: Admin sees full 17-item sidebar, Vendor sees restricted 2-item sidebar (My Portal, Settings).

issues: Console warnings about chart width/height being -1 - charts may not render properly on initial load (non-blocking)

tests: Impersonated token works for API calls

Modules: Dashboard, Clients, Transactions, Treasury, LP Management, Income & Expenses, Loans, O/S Accounts, PSP, Exchangers, Reconciliation, Audit, Logs, Reports, Approvals, Roles & Permissions, Settings
Vendor sees ONLY 2 sidebar items: My Portal, Settings
Vendor correctly redirected to /vendor-portal on login
Vendor does NOT see admin-only navigation items

critical_code_review_comments: Good: All 184+ routes now use require_permission(Module, Action) system
Good: Admin bypass works correctly for users with role='admin' and no role_id
Good: Vendor role has correct limited permissions: dashboard:view, transactions:view+approve, income_expenses:view+approve
Good: Frontend usePermissions hook correctly fetches /api/permissions/my and filters sidebar
Good: POST /api/transactions now requires authentication (security fix)
Good: Unauthenticated requests get 401 on all protected endpoints
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created - used existing users
context_for_next_testing_agent: RBAC Migration complete. Backend uses require_permission(Module, Action) decorator. Frontend uses usePermissions hook from /context/usePermissions.js to filter sidebar in Layout.js. Vendor role has dashboard:view, transactions:view+approve, income_expenses:view+approve. Admin role has all permissions. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_rbac_migration.py with 44 comprehensive tests covering admin access, vendor restrictions, vendor allowed endpoints, unauthenticated blocking, impersonation, and dashboard permissions.
